summary: aes-decode-runner-pro executes an obfuscated AES-encrypted payload at import time
details: On `require('aes-decode-runner-pro')`, index.js immediately calls `pkg.run()`, which routes through `runDefaultDecodedFunction` → `finalFinalDecodeAndRun` in src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js. That function AES-GCM-decrypts a multi-kilobyte hardcoded ciphertext (DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT in src/config/defaults.js) using a hardcoded passphrase ('default-dev-passphrase') and salt ('encode-npm-c-salt'), then evaluates the plaintext via `new Function("require", runnable)(require)` (custom-codec-pipeline.js:51). Any consumer who imports this package immediately runs opaque, attacker-controlled JavaScript with full `require` access in their own process. The package's stated purpose (a layered codec library / AES utility) is a cover for the dropper behavior — the name `aes-decode-runner-pro` and the encryption-with-shipped-key wrapping exist purely to evade scanner inspection of the executed code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
